DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF DANIEL LINLEY PLAINTIFF DANIEL LINLEY'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE NUMBER 15.1 IS GRANTED; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR ATTORNEY OF RECORD RICHARD DEWEY WHEELER, IN THE AMOUNT OF $567 IS DENIED CONDITIONALLY. DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE COMPLETE, AMENDED AND VERIFIED RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 THAT IDENTIFY THE FACTS, WITNESSES, AND DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT EACH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE WITHIN ONE WEEK OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. IF COMPLETE, VERIFIED RESPONSES ARE NOT RECEIVED IN PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S OFFICE WITHIN ONE WEEK OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER (E-SERVICE ACCEPTABLE), SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $567 ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL OF RECORD. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. THE PROPOSED ORDER SHALL BE EMAILED WITH APPROVAL SIGNATURE TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT THE EMAIL ADDRESS LISTED IN THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER C. CATALANOTTI; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (DISC302)

Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Courtney M. Brown of the law offices of Courtney M. Brown (415-371-9114) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Ryan Greenspan of the law offices of McNamara Ney Beatty Slattery et al (925-939-5330) appears as counsel for plaintiff/defendant/moving/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides in open court.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

PLAINTIFF DANIEL LINLEY Plaintiff Daniel Linley'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One Number 15.1 is GRANTED; Request For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Attorney Of Record Richard Dewey Wheeler, In The Amount Of $567 is DENIED conditionally. Defendant shall provide complete, amended and verified responses to Form Interrogatory 15.1 that identify the facts, witnesses, and documents that support each affirmative defense within one week of notice of this order. If complete, verified responses are not received in plaintiff's counsel's office within one week of notice of this order (e-service acceptable), sanctions in the amount of $567 are awarded against Defendants and their counsel of record. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312. The proposed order shall be emailed with approval signature to the judge pro tem at the email address listed in the tentative ruling.
